title: Social Behavior and Social Play
description: Skills related to social interaction, responding to and initiating mands with peers, and participating in games or social play.

title: Social Play 9-M
description: Spontaneously responds to the mands from peers 5 times (e.g., Pull me in the wagon. I want the train.). (E)
scoring_criteria: 1 point: Give the child 1 point if he spontaneously responds to the mands from peers 5 times during observation or testing. Do not give the child any points if an adult must prompt the behavior in any way. 1/2 point: Give the child 1/2 point if he spontaneously responds to the mands from peers 2 times during observation or testing.

title: Social Play 10-M
description: Spontaneously mands to peers to participate in games, social play, etc., 2 times (e.g., Come on you guys. Let's dig a hole.). (TO: 60 min.)
scoring_criteria: 1 point: Give the child 1 point if he spontaneously mands to peers to participate in games, social play, or other social activities 2 times during a 1-hour observation. 1/2 point: Give the child 1/2 point if he spontaneously mands to peers to participate in games, social play, or other social activities 1 time during a 1-hour observation.

title: Motor Imitation
description: Skills involving the ability to imitate the motor actions of others, progressing to specific object selection, fine motor skills, sequences, and generalized imitation.

title: Motor Imitation 6-M
description: Imitates 10 actions that require selecting a specific object from an array (e.g., selects a drumstick from an array also containing a horn and a bell, and imitates an adult's drumming). (T)
scoring_criteria: 1 point: Give the child 1 point if he imitates 10 actions with a specific object selected from an array of 3 when prompted, "Do this." 1/2 point: Give the child 1/2 point if he imitates 5 actions with a specific object selected from an array of 3 when prompted, "Do this."

title: Motor Imitation 7-M
description: Imitates 20 different fine motor actions when prompted, Do this (e.g., wiggling fingers, pinching, making a fist, making a butterfly).
scoring_criteria: 1 point: Give the child 1 point if he imitates 20 different fine motor actions when prompted, "Do this." 1/2 point: Give the child 1/2 point if he imitates 10 different fine motor actions when prompted, "Do this."

title: Motor Imitation 8-M
description: Imitates 10 different three-component sequences of actions when prompted, Do this (e.g., clapping, jumping, touching toes; pick up a doll, place her in a crib, and rock the crib). (T)
scoring_criteria: 1 point: Give the child 1 point if he imitates 10 three-component actions in contrived or natural settings modeled by a peer or an adult. 1/2 point: Give the child 1/2 point if he imitates 5 two-component actions in contrived or natural settings modeled by a peer or an adult.

title: Motor Imitation 9-M
description: Spontaneously, imitates 5 functional skills in the natural environment (e.g., eating with a spoon, putting on a coat, removing shoes). (O)
scoring_criteria: 1 point: Give the child 1 point if he spontaneously imitates 5 functional skills in the natural environment. 1/2 point: Give the child 1/2 point if he spontaneously imitates 2 functional skills in the natural environment.

title: Motor Imitation 10-M
description: Imitates (or attempts to with approximations) any novel motor action modeled by an adult with and without objects (i.e., a "generalized imitative repertoire"). (T)
scoring_criteria: 1 point: Give the child 1 point if he imitates (or approximates) many novel motor actions with and without objects modeled by an adult when prompted with, "Do this." 1/2 point: None.
